ops.text: A codex-wrapper agent launched an explicitly read-only adversarial review (round-4 refuter, question text says "no edits, no commits") with `--sandbox danger-full-access` instead of `--sandbox read-only` — while an unpushed 5-commit stack sat on the cc-steer tree and a live retrain was mid-flight. The brief's prose is not an enforcement boundary; the wrapper should pin the sandbox mode to match the task's declared access level (review/recon = read-only) rather than defaulting to full access. Caught by inspecting the process table mid-run. Wrappers need "review ⇒ --sandbox read-only" as a hard rule, not a convention.
